provisions.description: Grant of non-exclusive franchise to operate within Territory under Marks and System
Initial term: 10 years from Effective Date

Renewal: 5-year terms. Conditions: substantial compliance, current requirements met within 60 days, execute then-current FA + guaranty + confidentiality, general release, pay 15% renewal fee, no more than 3 defaults during term

Renewal agreement may differ materially from original, including increased royalty and ad fund. Territory boundaries preserved. Royalty will not exceed that imposed on similarly situated renewing franchisees.
Initial Franchise Fee: nonrefundable upon execution
Royalty Fee: 8% of Gross Revenues, $2,000/month minimum (waived first 12 months if good standing)
Administrative Fee: 11% of Gross Revenues to Rytech Admin Services
National Ad Fund: 1% of GR (max 3%)
Local Advertising: $1,000/month or 4% of GR, whichever greater
Software and Technology Fees: as set forth in Operations Manual
Rebuild Fees: 4% royalty + 4% admin + 1% ad fund on rebuild services
May provide Services only within Territory
National/regional account programs may override territory protection
Franchisor reserves right to reassign Program Accounts within Territory to other franchisees at sole discretion
Optional Programs may require additional fees, training, qualifications
Initial training mandatory for owner + 1 employee. Must complete to satisfaction. IICRC certifications required.
Must operate in strict compliance with Standards, Manual, and written directives
Required computer and technology systems as specified
Records and reporting: must provide information and reports as required, including carrier/customer data
Automatic termination (no notice): bankruptcy, insolvency, receivership, dissolution, levy
Curable defaults (with cure period): failure to report, train, comply with Manual, offer core services, attend meetings, background checks, pay, insure, license, meet sales requirement

Non-curable defaults (on notice): misuse marks, abandon business, illegal operation, material misrepresentation, unauthorized assignment, repeated non-compliance, felony conviction, unauthorized territory work
Abandonment defined as ceasing active operation for 7 consecutive business days
Repeated breach: 2 defaults of same type in 12 months, or 2 notices of default in 12 months, or 3 in 24 months (even if cured)
Post-termination: pay all amounts, return all materials, cease marks/trade secrets, cancel registrations, transfer phone numbers, de-identify, cease operations, assist in transition
12 months post-termination: may not sell/assign/transfer assets to Competitive Business
Must maintain accounts/records for 7 years after final payment
Attorney-in-fact appointment: franchisee irrevocably appoints franchisor to cancel registrations, disconnect phones, transfer URLs if not done within 2 days
Liquidated damages if franchisee continues using Marks or System after termination/expiration
During term: no involvement in Competitive Business; no diversion of carriers, customers, employees; no hiring of carrier reps or franchisor/affiliate/franchisee employees
Post-term (24 months): no Competitive Business within Territory, 10 miles of Territory border, any Rytech territory, or 10 miles of any Rytech territory. Same diversion/hiring restrictions.
Competitive Business defined broadly: any business deriving ANY revenues from Core Services or Additional Services, or any business offering similar franchises
Personal guaranty from all owners. Joint and several liability. Unlimited scope — guarantees all obligations under FA.
Waiver of punitive damages
Waiver of class actions
Georgia law governs guaranty
Spousal consent required — bound to non-compete, non-disclosure, dispute resolution, venue, governing law
Step 1: Internal dispute resolution — bring claim to franchisor's President
Step 2: Direct negotiations
Step 3: Non-binding mediation through JAMS (60-day limit). Costs split 50/50.

Step 4: Binding arbitration through JAMS. Single arbitrator (senior lawyer or retired judge with 5+ years franchise experience). Limited discovery (6 depositions/12hrs, 6 interrogatories, 6 doc requests). Arbitrator cannot: stay termination, assess punitive damages, modify agreement. Losing party pays all arbitration expenses.
Venue: city of franchisor's principal office (currently Cobb County, Georgia)
Governing law: Georgia (subject to state law)
GL $1M/$2M, Auto $1M, WC per state + $1M EL, CPL $1M (including mold, no sub-limits), Bailees $250K, Umbrella $1M/$2M recommended. Deductibles max $10K. Franchisor named as additional insured.
Franchisee must indemnify and hold harmless franchisor from all claims arising from franchisee's operation
11% admin fee to affiliated entity — the single largest ongoing fee and payable to CEO-controlled company
Franchisor reserves 'unequivocal right' to reassign Program Accounts at 'sole discretion'
All internet presence, email, URLs, domain names, social media controlled by franchisor. Franchisor owns all assigned email addresses.
Irrevocable power of attorney for post-termination de-identification (2 days to comply)
Minimum sales requirement: $500,000 by month 24 — failure is a curable default
Abandonment threshold: 7 consecutive business days of inactivity
